ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather data */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the weather data */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was collected */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the weather data (e.g., satellite, ground station) */,
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data format */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data type */,
  "wind_direction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the wind direction */,
  "barometric_pressure_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the barometric pressure */,
  "humidity_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the humidity level */,
  "precipitation_amount_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the precipitation amount */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Celsius */,
  "templow" REAL /* Low temperature in Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity as a percentage */,
  "barometric_pressure_value" REAL /* Barometric pressure in millibars */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_direction_value" REAL /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "precipitation_amount_value" REAL /* Precipitation amount in mm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_barometric_pressure_id FOREIGN KEY ("barometric_pressure_id") REFERENCES barometric_pressures ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("format_id") REFERENCES data_formats ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_precipitation_amount_id FOREIGN KEY ("precipitation_amount_id") REFERENCES precipitation_amounts ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_humidity_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("humidity_level_id") REFERENCES humidity_levels ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_wind_direction_id FOREIGN KEY ("wind_direction_id") REFERENCES wind_directions ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location in meters */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Time zone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather forecast */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather forecast */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the weather forecast */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the forecast is applicable */,
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data format */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data type */,
  "wind_direction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the wind direction */,
  "barometric_pressure_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the barometric pressure */,
  "humidity_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the humidity level */,
  "precipitation_amount_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the precipitation amount */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation amount in mm */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Cloud cover as a percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_humidity_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("humidity_level_id") REFERENCES humidity_levels ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_wind_direction_id FOREIGN KEY ("wind_direction_id") REFERENCES wind_directions ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("format_id") REFERENCES data_formats ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_precipitation_amount_id FOREIGN KEY ("precipitation_amount_id") REFERENCES precipitation_amounts ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_barometric_pressure_id FOREIGN KEY ("barometric_pressure_id") REFERENCES barometric_pressures ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the station is situated */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the weather station (e.g., surface, upper air) */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in meters */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Time zone of the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_stations_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the access event */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was accessed */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data type */,
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("format_id") REFERENCES data_formats ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_formats" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data format */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data format */,
  "file_extension" TEXT /* File extension for the data format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_alerts" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather alert */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather alert */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the weather alert */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the alert is applicable */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., storm, flood) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert becomes effective */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_alerts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather condition */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the weather condition */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the condition is applicable */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Type of the condition (e.g., sunny, cloudy) */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the condition */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the condition (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the condition becomes effective */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the condition expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_conditions_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wind_directions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wind direction */,
  "direction" TEXT /* Name of the wind direction */,
  "direction_description" TEXT /* Description of the wind direction */,
  "abbreviation" TEXT /* Abbreviation for the wind direction (e.g., N, S, E, W) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "barometric_pressures" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each barometric pressure */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Value of the barometric pressure */,
  "pressure_description" TEXT /* Description of the barometric pressure */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the barometric pressure (e.g., hPa, mmHg) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "humidity_levels" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each humidity level */,
  "level" REAL /* Value of the humidity level */,
  "level_description" TEXT /* Description of the humidity level */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the humidity level (e.g., %) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "precipitation_amounts" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each precipitation amount */,
  "amount" REAL /* Value of the precipitation amount */,
  "amount_description" TEXT /* Description of the precipitation amount */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the precipitation amount (e.g., mm, inches) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
